message:
refactor(e2e): migrate concurrent writer benchmark to asupersync runtime

The concurrent writer benchmark previously used raw std::thread::spawn
for its C SQLite WAL contention test. This migrates to the asupersync
blocking pool via a new BenchTask<T> abstraction that wraps
runtime.spawn_blocking() with an mpsc channel for result/panic
propagation.

spawn_bench_task() catches panics inside the worker closure and
forwards them through the channel, while BenchTask::wait() re-raises
any worker panic on the joining thread. The panic_payload_to_string()
helper downcasts Box<dyn Any + Send> to produce a readable message.

The barrier wait is moved before connection setup in each worker so
that one worker's setup failure cannot strand the others at the
barrier indefinitely.

New tests verify:
- spawn_bench_task runs the closure exactly once on the pool
- Worker panics surface through wait() with the original message

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
